title: About Juicebar
body_html:
Juicebar is a small cold-press and kombucha company. We make drinks we want to drink, and we sell them to people who want to drink them too.

Who we are

Nora Pell and Ben Ashworth started Juicebar in 2022 out of a converted garage. We had a hydraulic press, a leaky kombucha tank, and strong opinions about sugar.

Today there are twelve of us. We still press every bottle ourselves, we still brew kombucha for 14 days, and we still argue about recipes every Tuesday morning.

What we make

Three collections. Boosters — two-ounce concentrate shots. Kombuchas — 14-day small-batch brews. Cold-pressed — hydraulic-pressed raw juices. Plus bundles that pull them together.

Where we ship

Refrigerated overnight, anywhere in the lower 48. Live cultures in the kombucha, no preservatives in the juice — temperature control matters.

title: Contact us
body_html:
Questions about an order, wholesale inquiries, or something else? Drop us a note below and we'll reply within one business day.

For urgent order issues, call the kitchen at (555) 481-9922 weekdays, 9a–5p Pacific.

title: Sustainability
body_html:
We can't pretend that shipping refrigerated glass bottles across the country is environmentally free. We try to make the honest trade-offs.

Glass, not plastic

Every bottle is glass, and every bottle is reusable. Mail them back in the prepaid carton that came with your order, get a $0.25 credit per bottle on your next purchase.

Pulp goes to cows

We press about two tons of fruit pulp a week. Every bit of it goes to a dairy farm 40 minutes east of the kitchen — cows eat the sweet fruit pulp, the rest composts into soil.

Cold shipping is the honest cost

Overnight refrigerated shipping has a carbon cost we can't offset away. We buy offsets from Climeworks for 120% of our shipping emissions. It's not perfect. It's what we can do today.

What we still haven't figured out

The insulated liners inside every shipping carton are made of recycled denim, but the outer cooling gel packs are single-use plastic. We are looking for a better option.
